pattern: Since every path from corner to corner visits exactly the same number of cells, and each cell's cost is either 0 or 1, the total cost is bounded by the path length -- so tracking the best score reachable at each cell for every possible cost-so-far turns the search into a small table of running maxima.
hints: Every monotone path from the top-left to the bottom-right corner passes through exactly the same number of cells, so the total cost of any path can never exceed that fixed cell count regardless of which route you take.
For each cell, and for every possible total cost you could have spent getting there, keep the best score achievable -- when moving to a neighbor, that cost only ever grows by 0 or 1.

Fill this table row by row (or in any order where a cell's neighbors above and to the left are already finalized), then read the best score at the destination whose cost does not exceed k, or -1 if no cost bucket there was ever reached.

edge_inputs.description: single row, budget exactly enough for the costly cells
single cell grid, trivially reached with zero cost
budget of zero forces a path made only of zero-value cells
every non-start cell costs 1 and budget equals the minimum path length
the cheaper route is not the highest scoring one within budget
a route exists but every route exceeds the tiny budget
value-2 cells cost the same as value-1 cells, so preferring 2s never costs extra

stress.description: random grid values with a random budget, sized toward the constraint maximum
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 200))
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 2) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    grid[0][0] = 0
    k = rng.randint(0, m + n)
    return [grid, k]
